auth.loginSubtitle: Enter your credentials to access the portal
auth.loginError: Sign in failed. Please try again.
auth.sessionExpired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.

pki.title: Digital certificate verification
pki.subtitle: Hello {{name}}, please sign the challenge with your certificate.
pki.statusSelectCert: Select certificate and private key to sign the challenge
pki.devHint: Dev: select .crt/.pem and private .key files (Step CA). Production uses a USB token via the browser.
pki.certKeyRequired: Please select both certificate and private key files
pki.helpIntro: To sign in with a certificate you need the token driver and CA trust configured.
pki.helpStep1: Install the token driver (VNCA-link, BCYTTools, or your internal CA tooling).
pki.helpStep2: Import the root CA into the OS trust store.
pki.helpStep3: Insert the USB token and unlock the PIN when prompted.
pki.helpStep4: Choose the certificate that matches the account from step 1.
pki.signOutAndLogin: Sign out and return to login
pki.pendingSessionDesc: You signed in as {{name}} but have not verified your digital certificate yet.

adminSettings.title: Authentication settings
adminSettings.subtitle: Enable SSO/LDAP and configure your identity provider
adminSettings.secretHint: Leave blank to keep the current secret
adminSettings.ldapMigrateHint: On first LDAP enable, enter your current Portal password (e.g. Pass123!). Only users matching that password are written to LDAP and local login is disabled.
adminSettings.portalPasswordRequired: Required when enabling LDAP for the first time
adminSettings.portalPasswordHint: Must match the password used for admin / seeded users
adminSettings.pkiEnabledHint: When enabled, every user must verify a certificate after sign-in
adminSettings.pkiWarningDesc: Once enabled, no user (including admins) can bypass PKI at login. Ensure CA and test certificates are ready before piloting.
adminSettings.caUploadHint: Upload ca_bundle.crt (intermediate + root) or full CA chain — Step CA dev: portal/docker/auth-test/pki/ca_bundle.crt
adminSettings.caMissingDesc: Upload root_ca.crt before enabling PKI. Each organization uses its own CA.
adminSettings.aiEnabledHint: When off, template designers enter SQL manually (Gate 7).
adminSettings.aiApiKeyHint: Leave blank to keep the existing key

adminDepartments.title: Departments
adminDepartments.subtitle: Manage organizational units and their status
adminDepartments.name: Name
adminDepartments.codeFormat: Use 2–64 uppercase letters, digits, or underscores
adminDepartments.codeHint: Code is unique within your organization (e.g. KETOAN, NHANSU).
adminDepartments.empty: No departments yet. Create the first department to assign users.
adminDepartments.provisioningSuccess: Superset roles synced for this department
adminDepartments.provisioningFailed: Superset sync failed — retry from Settings or contact an administrator
adminDepartments.deactivateMessage: Deactivate {{name}} ({{code}})? Users keep their assignments but new ones cannot be added until reactivated.
adminDepartments.loadErrorHint: Your session may have expired. Try again or sign in again.

adminUsers.title: Users
adminUsers.subtitle: Manage portal users and department assignments

systemRoleDesc.tenant_admin: Configure SSO/PKI and manage departments and users
systemRoleDesc.cntt_lanhdao: Approve templates before sharing to departments
systemRoleDesc.dept_user: Submit export transactions using approved templates

adminUsers.passwordMin: Password must be at least 8 characters
adminUsers.deptPolicyHint: Department staff belong to one department only. Template designer and approver accounts do not require a department assignment.
adminUsers.loadErrorHint: Your session may have expired. Try again or sign in again.
adminUsers.deactivateMessage: Deactivate {{name}}? They will not be able to sign in until reactivated.
adminUsers.departmentPlaceholder: Select a department (create one first if empty)
adminUsers.setPasswordHint: Updates the login password (LDAP/Portal) and syncs it to Superset.
adminUsers.passwordUpdated: Password updated and Superset sync queued

platformTenants.title: Organizations
platformTenants.subtitle: Create tenants and assign an administrator for each company
platformTenants.name: Company name
platformTenants.adminPasswordMin: Password must be at least 8 characters
platformTenants.adminPasswordHint: Remember the exact password — use the same value on first login
platformTenants.successIntro: Save the login details below. The tenant admin must use the exact password entered during creation.
platformTenants.successPasswordWarning: Passwords are case-sensitive and special characters matter (e.g. @). If login fails, use the password copied here.

dashboard.title: Overview
dashboard.deptUnassigned: Your account is not assigned to a department yet. Contact an administrator to get access.
dashboard.loadError: Some overview figures could not be loaded. Try refreshing the page.

forbidden.title: Access denied
forbidden.subtitle: You do not have permission to view this page.

cnttTemplates.title: Export templates
cnttTemplates.subtitle: Design SQL export templates and submit them for IT leadership approval.
cnttTemplates.emptyAiOn: AI-assisted SQL generation is enabled for your organization.
cnttTemplates.emptyAiOff: Enter SQL manually — AI is disabled for your organization.

cnttApprovals.title: Template approval inbox
cnttApprovals.subtitle: Review pending export templates from template designers.
cnttApprovals.approveDesc: Choose which departments may access the dashboard on Superset after approval.
cnttApprovals.shareRequired: Select at least one department for SELECTED share mode.
cnttApprovals.approved: Template approved and shared with departments
cnttApprovals.rejectCommentRequired: A comment is required when rejecting
cnttApprovals.shareScopeHint: Choose departments allowed to view the dashboard on Superset and create export transactions. Specialists view only; leaders approve exports and download CSV/PDF.
cnttApprovals.pkiHintDesc: Approving will prompt for certificate signing when PKI is enabled.
cnttApprovals.pkiDesc: Sign the approval challenge with your client certificate.
cnttApprovals.pkiKeyRequired: Certificate and private key are required

templateStudio.nameRequired: Enter a template name before saving.
templateStudio.sqlRequired: Enter a SQL query before saving.
templateStudio.ownerOnlySubmit: Only the template author can submit for review.
templateStudio.previewEmpty: Run preview to see up to 100 sample rows.
templateStudio.previewMockHint: Sample data — connect Superset for live preview.
templateStudio.supersetHint: Push SQL to Superset to create a dataset, design a dashboard, then sync the link back to Portal before submitting.
templateStudio.pushDatasetFirst: Push SQL to Superset before starting design.
templateStudio.saveBeforePush: Save the template before pushing SQL.
templateStudio.saveBeforeSync: Save the template before syncing the dashboard.
templateStudio.dashboardPending: No dashboard linked yet — design on Superset, then sync.

ai.title: AI assistant
ai.subtitle: Describe the export you need; generated SQL is read-only (SELECT).
ai.promptPlaceholder: e.g. List export rows for the current tenant, limit 100
ai.disabledDesc: Your tenant administrator disabled AI. Enter SQL manually in the editor.

deptTemplates.title: Department templates
deptTemplates.subtitle: View dashboards on Superset (view-only). Create a transaction for leader-approved export.
deptTemplates.subtitleLeader: View and export directly on Superset. Approve specialist export requests on the Approvals page.
deptTemplates.empty: No templates shared with your department
deptTemplates.exportModalHint: Describe why you need this export so your leader can review before approving.
deptTemplates.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. Q2 report for the board
deptTemplates.transactionCreated: Transaction created — submit it from Transactions

deptTransactions.title: Export transactions
deptTransactions.subtitle: Specialists create and submit requests — leaders approve under Approvals & downloads.
deptTransactions.draftHint: Draft transactions are not visible to leaders. Click Submit for approval to add them to the queue.

deptApprovals.title: Export approval queue
deptApprovals.subtitle: Only transactions submitted by specialists (Submitted status) appear here.
deptApprovals.emptyHint: Ask the department specialist to click Submit for approval on the Export transactions page. Drafts do not appear here.

shareScope.selectPlaceholder: Select departments that may access this template
shareScope.summary: Template will be visible to {{count}} department(s)
